name: performance-profiling
description: Performance profiling principles. Measurement, analysis, and optimization techniques.
capabilities: Run Lighthouse performance audits via CLI script
Analyze Core Web Vitals metrics (LCP, INP, CLS)
Identify bundle size issues and optimization opportunities
Apply runtime profiling techniques using DevTools
Prioritize performance fixes for maximum impact
author: sickn33
securitySummary: Evaluated 11 static security findings. All patterns detected are false positives. The subprocess.run call uses list arguments (not shell=True), preventing command injection. Temp file operations use the safe tempfile module. Documentation examples in SKILL.md are not executable code. The 'cryptographic algorithm' alerts were misidentified keywords. This is a legitimate performance profiling skill with no security concerns.
valueStatement: This skill helps developers measure and optimize web application performance using Lighthouse audits and browser developer tools. It provides a structured workflow for identifying bottlenecks and improving Core Web Vitals scores.
limitations: Requires Lighthouse CLI to be installed separately
Cannot profile local development servers without running them
Performance results vary based on network conditions
Does not provide real-time monitoring in production

useCases.title: Frontend Developer Optimizing Load Speed
useCases.description: A frontend developer wants to improve their web app's loading performance. They use the skill to run Lighthouse audits, identify the biggest performance bottlenecks, and apply targeted optimizations.

useCases.title: DevOps Engineer Setting Up CI Performance Checks
useCases.description: A DevOps engineer wants to add automated performance testing to their CI/CD pipeline. They use the skill's Lighthouse script to run audits on each deployment and fail builds that fall below performance thresholds.

useCases.title: Full-Stack Developer Troubleshooting Runtime Issues
useCases.description: A full-stack developer notices slow interactions on their web app. They use the skill to profile runtime performance, identify long tasks, and find memory leaks using DevTools Memory tab.

promptTemplates.title: Basic Lighthouse Audit
promptTemplates.prompt: Run a Lighthouse performance audit on https://example.com using the performance-profiling skill. Show me the performance score and main recommendations.
promptTemplates.scenario: Quick performance check on a live website

promptTemplates.title: Analyze Core Web Vitals
promptTemplates.prompt: Using the performance-profiling skill, explain what Core Web Vitals (LCP, INP, CLS) measure and provide tips for improving each metric.

promptTemplates.title: Bundle Optimization Analysis
promptTemplates.prompt: Apply the performance-profiling skill to analyze a large JavaScript bundle. What common issues should I look for and what are the recommended optimization actions?

promptTemplates.title: Performance Workflow Implementation
promptTemplates.prompt: Using the 4-step profiling workflow from the performance-profiling skill (BASELINE, IDENTIFY, FIX, VALIDATE), help me optimize a slow web application. Start with establishing a baseline.

bestPractices: Always establish a performance baseline before making changes
Focus on the biggest bottleneck first rather than micro-optimizations
Validate improvements with repeated measurements to account for variance
antiPatterns: Guessing at performance problems without profiling first
Optimizing code before identifying the actual bottleneck
Ignoring real user data in favor of synthetic testing only

faq.question: What tools does this skill use?
faq.answer: This skill uses Lighthouse for performance auditing and browser DevTools for runtime profiling. Lighthouse CLI must be installed separately.

faq.question: Do I need to install any dependencies?
faq.answer: Yes, you need to install Lighthouse CLI globally with: npm install -g lighthouse

faq.question: Can this skill profile local websites?
faq.answer: Yes, you can run Lighthouse against localhost or any locally running development server.

faq.question: What are Core Web Vitals?
faq.answer: Core Web Vitals are Google's standardized metrics: Largest Contentful Paint (LCP), Interaction to Next Paint (INP), and Cumulative Layout Shift (CLS).

faq.question: How often should I run performance audits?
faq.answer: Run audits during development, before deployments, and regularly in production using Real User Monitoring (RUM).

faq.question: What if my performance score is poor?
faq.answer: Use the 4-step workflow: establish a baseline, identify the biggest bottleneck, make one targeted fix, then validate the improvement before moving to the next issue.
